segments.label: OVERDUB: the $9 robot stock (UMAC framing)

segments.quote_anchor: this is where I bought today at nine bucks... the probability of this thing going from 9 to 18 is greater than Nvidia going from 140 to 280

segments.label: WHAT HAPPENED: the 18-versus-7 race
segments.vo_script: TO WRITE from data-notes.md (verified): framed Dec 10 at nine twenty-eight close; catalyst (Don Jr. advisory news) disclosed; first touch of eighteen: December 27, twelve sessions later; first touch of seven: February 27, two months after the double. The asymmetric bet paid two to one before the stop was ever threatened.
segments.editorial_rules: frame-vs-price only; synth voice only; publish every outcome; catalysts disclosed; split-adjusted bars

segments.label: OVERDUB: the 10-day anchored VWAP as story trail

segments.quote_anchor: big thick Orange Line... the all-time mother of great signals

segments.label: WHAT HAPPENED: three endings from one entry (the trail applied to UMAC)
segments.vo_script: TO WRITE from data-notes.md epilogue (computed, method disclosed as daily typical-price approximation): strict one-touch trail exits December 19 at ten fifty-five, plus zero point seven eight R, before the big leg; the course's own re-enter discipline catches the re-cross December 20 and rides to January 7 at fourteen sixty-eight; no exit rule at all round-trips to six eighty-seven by late February. Same entry. Three exits. Three outcomes.
segments.animation_brief: Real UMAC bars with the AVWAP trail; three exit paths branch and land as a three-row table (animated stats table component).
segments.editorial_rules: frame-vs-price only; synth voice only; approximation method disclosed on screen

segments.quote_anchor: it broke through all three of the three five and 10 day anchored vwaps it came through the dragon that thing is right on the thin ice

segments.label: OVERDUB: cracks before the break (the live SPY framing)
segments.status: MAPPED (the prize; 2997 start adds the 'here's the other thing that broke today' setup; optional tail 3172-3197 = two range-stat days to 590)
segments.animation_brief: Keep his audio. Real SPY bars Dec 2024: 10d AVWAP cracks, 5-day rolls, 3-day down, SMA3-2h break; levels 597 / 602 / 592 land as he names them.
segments.quote_anchor: the 10day anchored vwap today that cracked and the five day rolled over and the 3-day is all pointing down... 592 is in range... you could see 592 by Friday

segments.label: WHAT HAPPENED: 592 by Friday
segments.vo_script: TO WRITE from data-notes.md Outcome 1 (verified): framed Tuesday December 10; by Friday the 13th, 592 never printed — the market bounced to 608 against the call first. The following Wednesday, on a scheduled Fed decision day, one session took out 597 and 592 in a single move. The structural read was right. The clock was wrong. Six sessions, not two.
segments.animation_brief: Real SPY bars Dec 10-20; the frame's levels persist; the failed-by-Friday beat lands BEFORE the FOMC-day resolution; catalyst chip on screen before the Dec 18 bar draws.
segments.editorial_rules: frame-vs-price only; synth voice only; publish every outcome; scheduled catalyst (FOMC 12/18) disclosed on screen

segments.label: Narrative decay precedes chart decay
segments.status: MAPPED (low-conf on card specifics — social/analyst tells are authored, footage is the kata-2 sharks read). Optional strong tail: 4793-4848 wrap ('Palantir cracked yesterday, it's a pure trade for me now... fully funded with profits')
segments.quote_anchor: when the buying is over and it just disappears that kata 2 is all of the Sharks still harvesting the failed dreams

segments.label: Reduce the stop + the simple rule (ATTRIBUTION DECISION REQUIRED)
segments.status: MAPPED to closing-range-30 (NOT the story-stocks session). ⚠ The lines are spoken by a COHORT MEMBER (the range-bar trader, 'move the stop to the elbow' exchange from ~2809s), but the published v1 cards attribute them as Ken's trail rule and Ken's confession.
segments.owner_decision: Three options: (a) keep the cut, re-card honestly as a cohort exchange Ken affirms; (b) replace with a Ken-voiced equivalent from the corpus (mine for his own reduce-the-stop / average-loss statement); (c) drop the beat and let an Ava explainer state the rule neutrally. v1's attribution should be corrected regardless of which.
segments.quote_anchor: you got to reduce the stop loss but make it simple... that made the average loss higher... you end up giving back more
